summary.intent: Implement a self-improving agent loop across three phases: autonomous skill creation (Phase A), agentic memory lifecycle (Phase B), and deployment story (Phase C), then improve the implementation with security fixes, metrics, and integration tests.
summary.outcome: All three phases were fully implemented and shipped: PatchSkillTool with 9 tests, memory prefetch/periodic extraction/session-end summary, VPS deployment templates, Settings UI controls for new config fields, Prometheus metrics instrumentation, and integration tests for silent turn variants — 35 files changed in a single commit.

repo: Provider registry is on the inner SharedState (behind RwLock), not directly on GatewayState — access pattern is `state.inner.read().await.llm_providers`
The web UI's /new and /reset dispatch the Command hook from JavaScript, not from Rust — the Command hook has no Rust dispatch site in production code
memory_write_mode_allows_save and AgentScopedMemoryWriter were pub(crate) in the chat crate, requiring visibility promotion and re-export from lib.rs to be accessible from the gateway crate
default_true() function in schema.rs is private and NOT accessible via `use super::*` in submodules — each module needing it must define its own local copy
The `metrics` feature is NOT in chat crate's default features but IS enabled transitively through the gateway's default features
Periodic memory extraction needed a cloned provider Arc before run_with_tools consumes it — the provider is moved into the async block at the call site
ChatRuntime trait does not expose a provider registry — providers must be resolved from GatewayState or passed explicitly
The `run_streaming` path (for models without tool support) was completely bypassed by memory prefetch — a parallel code path that mirrors run_with_tools
SessionMemoryHook (in plugins crate) fires on Command events from the frontend but has no LLM provider access, making LLM-powered session summaries impossible from within the hook system

code.finding: Post-turn periodic extraction block — provider must be cloned before run_with_tools consumes it, and collapsible nested if blocks trigger clippy::collapsible_if
GatewayState has memory_manager directly, but llm_providers is on inner SharedState behind RwLock
format_recalled_context must XML-escape memory content to prevent prompt injection via crafted memory entries containing </recalled_context> tags
PatchSkillTool needed symlink confinement via canonicalize() + symlink_metadata() check matching the pattern already used in DeleteSkillTool and WriteSkillFilesTool

Refactored into run_silent_memory_turn_with_prompt accepting a SilentTurnPrompt enum — original function delegates for backward compatibility; requires #[derive(Debug)] on enum for #[tracing::instrument]

workflow: Collapsing nested `if let Some(x)` blocks (clippy::collapsible_if) can introduce brace-count bugs — removing the inner block removes one level of nesting but all closing braces remain until manually deleted
When promoting pub(crate) items to pub for cross-crate access, also update the crate's lib.rs re-exports to make them part of the public API surface
Disk space issues during cargo test are a real blocker — running tests per-crate rather than workspace-wide avoids filling /tmp with build artifacts
Metrics macros in a feature-gated block require conditional use imports (#[cfg(feature = "metrics")] use ...) alongside the gated call sites, otherwise clippy flags unnecessary qualification

friction: Brace mismatch after collapsing nested if blocks (clippy::collapsible_if) — required multiple read/edit cycles to count braces correctly
Provider access required navigating three layers: GatewayState → inner (RwLock<SharedState>) → llm_providers, which was not obvious from the surface API

The Command hook (SessionMemoryHook) is dispatched from JavaScript, not Rust — discovering this required extensive grepping and reading multiple dispatch paths before confirming no Rust dispatch site exists
default_true() visibility — spent time removing and re-adding it after discovering it's private and not re-exported via use super::*
run_streaming path was a silent gap — the streaming code path is parallel to run_with_tools but receives persona.memory_text directly, making the prefetch bypass non-obvious
clippy unnecessary_qualification in chat crate for metrics macros — required adding conditional use imports rather than using fully-qualified paths
ProviderRegistry type annotation failures — type inference on RwLock read guards required explicit type annotations that weren't obvious from the API
open_items: moltis setup CLI wizard (3.3) — explicitly deferred; web UI onboarding + env vars already cover the use case for headless VPS setups
Session-end summary is not wired to channel-based /new and /reset (only web UI sessions.reset RPC) — channel commands go through a different code path that doesn't call the gateway session service
No mock-provider integration tests for periodic extraction and session summary — these require a test LlmProvider that returns scripted tool calls for memory writes
Prometheus metrics for skill creation events (patch_skill calls, write_skill_files calls) — only silent turn and prefetch are currently instrumented
Memory prefetch is disabled for messages under 10 chars and / commands but no metrics track skip-rate — useful for tuning the threshold
McpServerEntry display_name fix was a pre-existing bug found incidentally — no test was added to prevent regression in the schema validator
